Potential runtime crash: if `evt.destinationCalendar` is set to null, calling `.push()` on it will throw; it should be initialized to an array or guarded

Error handling rethrows with a generic "Unable to load users" message, losing specific context (e.g., missing/empty `dynamicUserList` or `eventType.hosts`), making debugging harder; should preserve or specialize messages

augment.text: `mainHostDestinationCalendar` can be undefined when `evt.destinationCalendar` is missing/empty, so accessing `mainHostDestinationCalendar.integration` can throw at runtime (Meet fallback can crash for events without a destination calendar).

If `reference.credentialId` points to a missing/removed credential and the DB lookup doesn’t return an app slug, `credential` can remain falsy and `updateEvent(credential, ...)` will throw; the “credential not found” case isn’t handled before calling `updateEvent`.

`selectedCalendar` can be undefined when no destination calendar matches `credentialId`, but later code uses it directly (e.g., `calendar.events.patch({ calendarId: selectedCalendar, ... })`), leading to invalid Google API calls or missed patching when falling back to `primary` for the initial insert.

When `externalCalendarId` is falsy, the fallback `find((cal) => cal.externalId === externalCalendarId)` compares against undefined, so `selectedCalendar`/`calendarId` often becomes undefined; this can cause updates to target an invalid calendarId and deletes to fall back to `primary` instead of the booking’s actual destination calendar.

The feature-flag conditional for setting `slug`/`requestedSlug` appears inverted: when `IS_TEAM_BILLING_ENABLED` is false, neither `slug` nor `requestedSlug` is set, potentially creating org records without a slug in the non-team-billing flow.

propel.text: Accessing mainHostDestinationCalendar.integration can throw when evt.destinationCalendar is undefined or an empty array, causing events without a destination calendar to crash before the Cal Video fallback runs; use optional chaining/guard the access.

baz.text: CalendarService.createEvent destructures only the first destinationCalendar and always posts the event to that single calendar, ignoring additional destinationCalendar entries (collective host destinations)
As a result, Office365 events are created only on the main/first host instead of per host credential/externalId-matched destination calendar
When IS_TEAM_BILLING_ENABLED is false, organization creation skips persisting both slug and metadata.requestedSlug, leaving new orgs without any slug value
Downstream routes/createTeams that require team.slug or metadata.requestedSlug will break for non-billing orgs because the slug is not saved at creation

`GoogleCalendarService.updateEvent` lacks `credentialId` in scope, so it cannot correctly select the appropriate destination calendar when multiple hosts have different calendars; the API needs a way to identify the correct calendar during updates

In `GoogleCalendarService.deleteEvent` (or similar), the `.find()` condition is similarly flawed (effectively comparing `externalCalendarId` with itself), so it fails to locate the correct calendar and should instead use `externalCalendarId` directly or a valid fallback
